mnemonics.title: Big O Big Omega Big Theta
mnemonics.content:
O = Upper (O stands for 'On' - you're on a budget, can't exceed)
Ω = Lower (Ω looks like upside down U - the floor/lower bound)
Θ = Tight (Θ has lines on top and bottom - sandwiched/tight)

mnemonics.title: Complexity Growth Order
mnemonics.content:
I Love N log N (in order of growth)
I = O(1) - Index/Lookup
L = O(log n) - Logarithmic
N = O(n) - Linear
log N = O(n log n) - Linearithmic
N² = O(n²) - Quadratic
N³ = O(n³) - Cubic
2^N = O(2ⁿ) - Exponential
N! = O(n!) - Factorial

mnemonics.title: Omega Omega
mnemonics.content:
Ω = Omega = 'At Least' - like the minimum you can expect
O = Oscar = 'At Most' - like the maximum budget
Θ = Theta = 'Exactly' - right in the middle

acronyms.usage: Used to express worst-case time complexity. f(n) = O(g(n)) means f grows no faster than g.
Used to express best-case time complexity. f(n) = Ω(g(n)) means f grows at least as fast as g.
Used when upper and lower bounds are the same. f(n) = Θ(g(n)) means f grows exactly as g.
f(n) = o(g(n)) means f grows strictly slower than g.
f(n) = ω(g(n)) means f grows strictly faster than g.

visualTips.visualization: Think of O as a ceiling - you're bounded from above. Draw an O-shaped bowl with the function inside.
Ω is the floor - bounded from below. Think of Ω as an upside-down bowl, supporting the function from underneath.
Θ is sandwiched - function fits exactly between upper and lower bounds. Like a function between two horizontal lines.
Draw a graph with n on x-axis and time on y-axis. Steeper curve = worse complexity. O(1) is flat, O(n) diagonal, O(n²) parabola, O(2ⁿ) shoots up vertically.

analogies.analogy: Big-O is like the maximum speed limit on a highway. Your actual speed might be lower, but it will never exceed this bound.
Big-Omega is like the minimum time it takes to complete a task. Even in the best conditions, it takes at least this long.
Big-Theta is like the exact weight of a package - it must be at least X and at most X, meaning exactly X.
Opening a safe with the correct code - instant, doesn't matter how big the safe is.
Finding a word in a dictionary by opening to the middle, then the middle of remaining section - eliminates half each time.
Reading a book from page 1 to page n - takes time proportional to number of pages.
Comparing every student with every other student in a class - nested pairings.
Trying every possible password combination where each position has 2 options - grows explosively.
